You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 87153ae1).

## Issue to Resolve

Formatting enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas causes rule violations on first run
## Expected Behavior
<!---Tell us what should happen. -->
<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->

When formatting and validating the following enum class, the ktlint command should exit successfully (status `0`):

```kotlin
enum class TestEnum() {
    A,
    B;

    companion object {
        const val A_NUMBER = 1L
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
<!---Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- Provide the exact command which was executed but please -->
<!--- ensure that the flag "--log-level=debug" is added to the -->
<!--- command as well. Provide the output of this command. -->

When running ktlint on the above enum class, it is formatted correctly, but the run fails with rule violations (exit status `1`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format 
17:25:00.694 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:1:1: Missing trailing comma before "}" (trailing-comma-on-declaration-site)
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:5:4: Unnecessary semicolon (no-semi)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  no-semi: 1
  trailing-comma-on-declaration-site: 1
Exit status: 1 
```

I note that the line number for the `no-semi` violation is the line number after formatting.

A second run on the (now properly formatted) code runs correctly (exit status `0`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format
17:25:05.397 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
Exit status: 0
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run command on above enum class:
```ktlint --format```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I have the same issue

See release notes of 0.48.0:
> * The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))

@paul-dingemans I don't quite follow. My issue is that after doing formatting, ktlint reports invalid  `no-semi` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` rule violations. A subsequent run of ktlint (correctly) passes the same code.

Ah now I see. Although it is not visual in the textual representation of the code, the trailing comma is not inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code. I will try to fix it.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `RELEASING.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
**Exploring Kotlin enums**

I'm considering the structure of Kotlin enums, where entries are separated by commas and a semicolon follows if there are members. It seems like a trailing comma might be allowed after the last enum entry, like after B, but I'm not totally sure. The syntax looks like `enum class E { A, B; fun foo(){} }`, where the semicolon indeed separates entries from members. I wonder if a trailing comma is permitted before a semicolon. Kotlin grammar suggests it could be allowed, but I need to clarify.**Investigating Kotlin trailing commas**

I'm considering whether Kotlin allows an optional trailing comma before a semicolon. I think there might be formatting quirks in IntelliJ with entries like `A, B, ;`. It seems that after formatting, rule violations appear only on the first run, while the second run passes without issues. This might indicate that the trailing comma rule sees a missing comma in the AST due to the semicolon. I should check how KtLint processes these sequentially and what modifications are reflected in the AST as a result. Let's inspect the relevant rule further.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ARROW
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.CLASS
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUNCTION_TYPE
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.isCodeLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.lineIndent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.noNewLineInClosedRange
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.prevCodeLeaf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import kotlin.properties.Delegates
 25→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 26→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtClass
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCollectionLiteralExpression
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDestructuringDeclaration
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtEnumEntry
 35→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunctionLiteral
 36→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtParameterList
 37→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtPsiFactory
 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtValueArgumentList
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenEntry
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenExpression
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.anyDescendantOfType
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.collectDescendantsOfType
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.nextLeaf
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.parents
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.prevLeaf
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.cast
 47→
 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,

441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→

1→# How to make new project release
 2→
 3→### Publishing a new release
 4→
 5→Note: These steps should be done directly in the pinterest/ktlint repository, not in your personal fork.
 6→
 7→1. Create a new branch (e.g. `0.50.0-prep`)
 8→2. Update `VERSION_NAME` with the new release version in the project root `gradle.properties`. Be sure to remove `-SNAPSHOT`.
 9→3. Update `CHANGELOG.md` to rename the `Unreleased` section to the new release name, following the `## [x.x.x] - YYYY-MM-DD` format.
10→4. Add the new release to the bottom of the `CHANGELOG.md` file.
11→5. Push your changes to the branch, and merge it to `master`.
12→6. Update your local `pinterest/ktlint` `master` branch; verify you see the `gradle.properties` and `CHANGELOG.md` changes locally.
13→7. Add a tag with the new release version, and push it directly to remote (e.g. `git tag 0.50.0 && git push origin 0.50.0`). This will kick off the [Release workflow](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/actions/workflows/release.yml).
14→9. Close and release the repo on Sonatype. (Only Pinterest employees can do this.)
15→10. Find the `<release>-update-refs` branch in the repo (created by the `.announce` script) and merge it.
16→11. Update `gradle.properties` with the new `SNAPSHOT` version, and add a new empty `Unreleased` section to the top of `CHANGELOG.md` and commit. (This can be done directly in the main repo or in your fork.)
17→

However, they expect a 0 exit status after formatting, which adds some confusion, especially since the first run can show errors that weren't resolved. I need to reconcile this with the documentation that states it should return 0 if all errors are corrected. There seems to be some inconsistency here!**Analyzing ktlint exit codes**

I think ktlint should return 0 if all errors could be autocorrected. The fact that a subsequent run with the same file returns 0 suggests there were no errors. The first run showing an exit of 1 likely means some errors remained after formatting, perhaps due to issues in the AST. Even if autocorrect makes changes, errors are still reported. If the CLI expects an exit of 1 for any initial problems, that could explain some confusion. I should check the exit behavior in version 0.49 to clarify things!

 72→     * [script] true if this is a Kotlin script file
 73→     * [debug] True if invoked with the --debug flag
 74→     * [editorConfigDefaults] contains default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in
 75→     * any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the [fileName]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults]
 76→     * this takes precedence above the default values defined in the KtLint project.
 77→     * [editorConfigOverride] should contain entries to add/replace from loaded `.editorconfig` files. If a property is
 78→     * set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same property being set in any other way.
 79→     *
 80→     * For possible keys check related [Rule]s that implements [UsesEditorConfigProperties] interface.
 81→     *
 82→     * For values use `PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid("override", <expected type>)` approach.
 83→     * It is also possible to set value into "unset" state by using [PropertyType.PropertyValue.UNSET].
 84→     *
 85→     * [isInvokedFromCli] **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool.
 86→     * Enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin Compiler initialization.
 87→     * Usually you don't need to use it and most probably it will be removed in one of next versions.
 88→     */
 89→    @Deprecated("Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49")
 90→    public data class ExperimentalParams(
 91→        val fileName: String? = null,
 92→        val text: String,
 93→        val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 94→        val userData: Map<String, String> = emptyMap(),
 95→        val cb: (e: LintError, corrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96→        val script: Boolean = false,
 97→        val debug: Boolean = false,
 98→        val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 99→        val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
100→        val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
101→    ) {
102→        internal val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner> =
103→            ruleProviders
104→                .map { RuleRunner(it) }
105→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
106→                .toSet()
107→
108→        internal fun getRules(): Set<Rule> =
109→            ruleRunners
110→                .map { it.getRule() }
111→                .toSet()
112→
113→        init {
114→            require(ruleProviders.any()) {
115→                "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
116→            }
117→            // Extract all default and custom ".editorconfig" properties which are registered
118→            val editorConfigProperties =
119→                getRules()
120→                    .asSequence()
121→                    .filterIsInstance<UsesEditorConfigProperties>()
122→                    .map { it.editorConfigProperties }
123→                    .flatten()
124→                    .plus(DEFAULT_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTIES)
125→                    .map { it.name }
126→                    .distinct()
127→                    .toSet()
128→
129→            userData
130→                .keys
131→                .intersect(editorConfigProperties)
132→                .let {
133→                    check(it.isEmpty()) {
134→                        "UserData should not contain '.editorconfig' properties ${it.sorted()}. Such properties " +
135→                            "should be passed via the 'ExperimentalParams.editorConfigOverride' field. Note that this is " +
136→                            "only required for properties that (potentially) contain a value that differs from the " +
137→                            "actual value in the '.editorconfig' file."
138→                    }
139→                }
140→
141→            userData
142→                .keys
143→                .minus(editorConfigProperties)
144→                .let {
145→                    check(it.isEmpty()) {
146→                        "UserData contains properties ${it.sorted()}. However, userData is deprecated and will be " +
147→                            "removed in a future version. Please create an issue that shows how this field is " +
148→                            "actively used."
149→                    }
150→                }
151→        }
152→
153→        internal val normalizedFilePath: Path?
154→            get() = if (fileName == STDIN_FILE || fileName == null) {
155→                null
156→            } else {
157→                Paths.get(fileName)
158→            }
159→
160→        internal val isStdIn: Boolean get() = fileName == STDIN_FILE
161→    }
162→
163→    /**
164→     * Check source for lint errors.
165→     *
166→     * Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49. The static object [KtLint] is replaced by class [KtLintRuleEngine]. Main
167→     * difference is that the class needs to be instantiated once with the [KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration] and is reused
168→     * for all subsequent calls. The call to [KtLintRuleEngine.lint] only takes a reference to the code that needs to be
169→     * linted instead of all configuration parameter as well.
170→     *
171→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
172→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
173→     */
174→    @Deprecated("Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49. See changelog or KDOC for migration to KtLintRuleEngine.")
175→    public fun lint(params: ExperimentalParams) {
176→        KtLintRuleEngine(
177→            params.ruleProviders,
178→            params.editorConfigDefaults,
179→            params.editorConfigOverride,
180→            params.isInvokedFromCli,
181→        ).lint(
182→            Code.CodeSnippetLegacy(
183→                content = params.text,
184→                fileName = params.fileName,
185→                script = params.script,
186→                isStdIn = params.isStdIn,
187→            ),
188→        ) { params.cb(it, false) }
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Fix style violations.
193→     *
194→     * Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49. The static object [KtLint] is replaced by class [KtLintRuleEngine]. Main
195→     * difference is that the class needs to be instantiated once with the [KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration] and is reused
196→     * for all subsequent calls. The call to [KtLintRuleEngine.format] only takes a reference to the code that needs to
197→     * be formatted instead of all configuration parameter as well.
198→     *
199→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
200→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
201→     */
202→    @Deprecated("Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49. See changelog or KDOC for migration to KtLintRuleEngine.")
203→    public fun format(params: ExperimentalParams): String =
204→        KtLintRuleEngine(
205→            params.ruleProviders,
206→            params.editorConfigDefaults,
207→            params.editorConfigOverride,
208→            params.isInvokedFromCli,
209→        ).format(
210→            Code.CodeSnippetLegacy(
211→                content = params.text,
212→                fileName = params.fileName,
213→                script = params.script,
214→                isStdIn = params.isStdIn,
215→            ),
216→        ) { lintError, autocorrected -> params.cb(lintError, autocorrected) }
217→
218→    /**
219→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
220→     */

content.thinking:
**Analyzing semicolon formatting**

I'm realizing that if a semicolon is on its own line, its parent might actually be CLASS_BODY rather than ENUM_ENTRY. However, the no-semi rule indicates that the semicolon is a direct child of the enum entry. It seems like the AST implies that the semicolon token is always a child of the enum entry—even if it appears on a separate line. I'm trying to figure out how formatting affects this and how shifts in structure could occur in the code.**Examining semicolon treatment**

I’m noticing that the algorithm changes the AST, making the semicolon a sibling, which the no-semi rule sees as unnecessary. I think the no-semi rule runs after the trailing comma rule in the CLI, which might incorrectly remove the semicolon. I'm puzzled why it doesn't consider the semicolon between enum entries and following declarations necessary. It appears to only flag semicolons that are direct children of enum entries. I suspect that the no-semi rule should be updated to recognize semicolons that terminate an enum entry list in the class body as necessary, especially since they are important for members in enums.**Clarifying semicolon relationships**

In PSI, I'm considering that the semicolon might actually be a sibling rather than a child. Even without the trailing comma correction, typical code like `B; fun ...` might have the semicolon as a sibling. But I realize the no-semi rule ignores the semicolon when its treeParent is of type ENUM_ENTRY, and additional declarations follow. This suggests the semicolon under the enum entry is treated as necessary unless it becomes a sibling, potentially introducing a bug. It seems that the actual Kotlin parser might produce the semicolon inconsistently based on its placement in relation to enum entries.**Investigating semicolon behavior**

I'm thinking that after the trailing comma rule, the semicolon might want to be on a separate line, and to get it there, it gets moved out, causing no-semi to remove it. This could definitely lead to issues. On the second run, since the semicolon is already on its own line, the trailing comma rule doesn't move it again. However, it seems that if no semicolon is there, the code may still pass without complaints, suggesting that maybe the semicolon is indeed unnecessary with a trailing comma and companion object.**Exploring enum semicolon requirements**

I'm wondering whether a semicolon is required after an enum when there's a function following it. The test related to Issue 1609 suggests that a semicolon needs to be on a separate line when a trailing comma is inserted and a function is after it. This implies that semicolons might still be needed in some cases, even with trailing commas. If the no-semi rule removes semicolons, it could break important cases. I think I'll try running a quick parser using a small Kotlin file, but if it doesn't compile, I might turn to ktlint. It might be simpler to write a unit test that uses both the TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule and NoSemicolonsRule to verify formatting and ensure no lint errors persist afterward. I'll check KtLintAssertThat for how it handles these conditions.

**Clarifying code formatting rules**

I want to ensure the enums are presented with each entry on a separate line, and the semicolon must be on the same line as the last entry. The code currently shows A, B; separated by newlines, but I think we can apply the same logic about trailing commas. It seems I might need to move the semicolon to its own line, especially since the tests show that the formatted output requires a newline. Additionally, I might look into removing empty parentheses in the enum class.**Determining enum class formatting**

I think the enum class can keep the parentheses, but the indentation rule might actually remove them. I shouldn't change the input to fit that. Now, I'm considering whether there should be a blank line between the semicolon and the companion object. In the trailing comma tests, they don't use a blank line, but the input does have one. The sample code shows a blank line between B; and the companion object, so I might keep that, but I need to be cautious about shifting it if the semicolon moves.**Investigating trailing comma issue**

I think the `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` function may return the last `}` node due to not finding a semicolon after the last enum entry when it's on the same line. However, existing tests for `TRIANGLE;` are passing, so that doesn’t seem to be the root issue. The error message is there because `inspectNode` refers to `}` instead of the semicolon. Perhaps the presence of empty constructor parentheses like in `TestEnum()` alters the parse tree. However, I need to ensure that the semicolon should still be recognized as a child of the enum entry.**Examining enum class behavior**

I’m wondering if the presence of primary constructor parentheses impacts how enum class body children are handled. It might be the case that the last enum entry doesn’t have a trailing comma, but it's followed by a semicolon, newline, and companion object. In a test case where there’s an enum without a trailing comma followed by other declarations, they expect a comma added before the semicolon. This test currently passes, suggesting that the rules are functioning correctly. However, the issue still hangs around since the rule engine marks the trailing comma violation as not corrected.**Troubleshooting trailing comma issues**

I'm noticing that the violation messages indicate issues with trailing commas and missing semicolons, which suggests that the trailing comma violation might not have been corrected properly. The corrected file seemingly has a trailing comma, but the flag indicating correction could still be false. Perhaps this happens if a violation is emitted while the correction actually fails due to a bug. If the user reports the file formatted correctly, the corrected flag should be true. Yet, if the AST doesn’t reflect the inserted comma, it could cause confusion— I'll inspect the internal rule engine to understand how the corrected flag is determined.

 60→         * before visiting the nodes.

 63→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {

 66→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 67→                }
 68→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {

 72→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, fqRuleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 73→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(

content.thinking:
**Reviewing Kotlin string formatting**

I’m analyzing the use of backslash escapes in Kotlin strings, specifically `\"` to represent `"`. It’s possible that file interpretation by ktlint is causing issues. The compile error suggests unexpected tokens due to newlines in strings or perhaps a trailing comma after the argument in the `describedAs(` method. I wonder if trailing commas are allowed. Compile errors state an expectation for `"` which could relate to escaping issues. Inside the `joinToString` lambda, it seems I'm using escaped quotes incorrectly.**Examining string issues in Kotlin**

I’m looking at an outer string that starts on line 49 and continues on line 50. In the Kotlin string literal, there are `\"` sequences to create quotes, which typically work. I’m wondering if Kotlin doesn't allow `\"` within string templates, but it seems like it should be fine. The compile errors could stem from using a property that doesn’t exist in LintError. I need to check if `ruleId` is indeed a valid property; it’s essential to clarify that!**Analyzing Kotlin string template errors**

I’m thinking through a compiler error that mentions unresolved references, which might suggest broken parsing due to nested `${...}` confusion. In the string `"Reported errors were: ${lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo.joinToString { \"${it.first.ruleId} (corrected=${it.second})\" }}"`, the outer `${...}` contains inner templates. This should be fine, but it seems the parser gets confused when there are nested `${` within a string literal. The braces matching rules could be causing issues that I need to clarify further.**Simplifying error handling in Kotlin**

I think it should be fine, but I’m considering whether Kotlin allows `\"${` within string templates due to escaping issues. To keep things simple, I’ll avoid nested templates altogether. Instead, I can assert that `uncorrectedErrors` is empty and use `.withFailMessage` to display the whole list if it fails. 

I’ll edit the test to use `assertThat(uncorrectedErrors).describedAs("... $lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo").isEmpty()`. Simplifying the message to `"Reported errors were: $lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo"` helps avoid using `joinToString`. Now I’ll patch the test file accordingly.

content.thinking:
**Evaluating code options**

I’ve figured out the problem involves the `filename` rule. Using CodeSnippet without a specified filename defaults to "File.kt," while the rule expects "TestEnum.kt." This is unrelated to the issue and results in corrected=false. I’m considering whether to exclude that rule or supply a filename in CodeSnippet. Using CodeSnippetLegacy is an option, but since it's deprecated, I think the better idea is to use Code.CodeSnippet with script set to false and configure the engine with an editor config override.**Adjusting test setup**

I’m thinking about adjusting my test by creating a temporary file called "TestEnum.kt" that contains the code plus a newline. The engine will format this using Code.CodeFile(tempFile.toFile()), which will also load the editorconfig from the filesystem, and that should be fine. I need to remember to delete the file afterward, though. I'll use JUnit's temp directory or Kotlin's createTempFile for this. Using Code.CodeFile will read content at creation and ensure the correct file name, so it should satisfy the filename rule, which is great.**Updating test setup**

I need to update the test by using `java.nio.file.Files.createTempDirectory` and resolving the path to "TestEnum.kt". I'll write the code string to this file, including a newline. Then I’ll format the code and capture any lint errors. It’s important to note that the format through `Code.CodeFile(File)` returns formatted text as a string rather than writing back to the file, which is fine. I’ll ensure the content includes a final newline to avoid any `final-newline` issues, then proceed with the update.

{"file_path":"/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/EnumTrailingCommaWithMemberDeclarationsFormattingTest.kt","old_string":"        val ruleProviders = StandardRuleSetProvider().getRuleProviders()
        val ruleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(ruleProviders = ruleProviders)

        val lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo = mutableListOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
        val formattedCode =
            ruleEngine.format(Code.CodeSnippet(code)) { lintError, corrected ->
                lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo.add(lintError to corrected)
            }","new_string":"        val ruleProviders = StandardRuleSetProvider().getRuleProviders()
        val ruleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(ruleProviders = ruleProviders)

        val tempDir = kotlin.io.path.createTempDirectory(prefix = "ktlint-")
        val kotlinFile = tempDir.resolve("TestEnum.kt")
        kotlin.io.path.writeText(kotlinFile, code + "\n")

        val lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo = mutableListOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
        val formattedCode =
            ruleEngine.format(Code.CodeFile(kotlinFile.toFile())) { lintError, corrected ->
                lintErrorsWithCorrectionInfo.add(lintError to corrected)
            }"}

content.thinking:
**Reviewing test outcomes**

I see all standard tests passed quickly, which means they probably executed successfully. It says one was executed, and the rest are up-to-date, possibly using previous results. Now, I should compile the changes and maybe run `./gradlew test` fully, even if it takes longer. I’ll check modifications in NoSemicolonsRule, which now detects semicolons after the last enum entry while ignoring optional trailing commas. Also, our updates for special cases should help prevent issues during the second lint stage. The earlier test had only trailing comma and final newline corrections, with no uncorrected errors.**Addressing comma rule issues**

I'm considering whether we still have an issue where the trailing comma rule might emit a missing comma before "}" when a semicolon isn't found. But it seems fixed with the `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` method since it now finds semicolons even as siblings. I should also check our scanning loop that uses `nextCodeLeaf` from the last enum entry leaf, as this may skip commas and could potentially lead to incorrect returns. I also want to adjust the imports in the NoSemicolonsRule file and ensure there aren’t unused imports in the TrailingComma file. Good to note that our changes now consider semicolons that terminate enum entries.**Finalizing enum semicolon handling**

For enums with member declarations, if the semicolon isn't last, it returns false, so the no-semi will not complain. If an enum has no members but a separate semicolon node in the class body, it will be removed. The `isEnumEntriesTerminatingSemicolon` check skips commas and will correctly identify if the semicolon is the last code leaf. I need to adjust imports in NoSemicolonsRule since `val prevCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()` is unused and triggering a warning, but it’s not critical. Now, I’ll summarize the fix and ensure everything is resolved for the user.**Confirming testing protocols**

I need to ensure that we haven't violated the instruction to create only new test files, and it seems we did that correctly. There's been no modification to existing test files apart from adding new ones. Also, I haven’t executed any git commands, which aligns with the guidelines. Everything checks out, so I can confidently finalize this! It looks like we're in the clear and ready to wrap things up.

content.text:
Implemented a fix for the “enum + member declarations + missing trailing comma” first-run failure by making the AST stable after autocorrection:

- **`TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule`** now correctly finds the enum-entry terminating **semicolon even after it has been moved outside the last enum entry** (e.g. after autocorrect), instead of falling back to `}` and re-reporting a missing trailing comma.
- **`NoSemicolonsRule`** now recognizes that such a semicolon (when it terminates the enum entries list) is **not removable** unless it’s truly redundant (i.e. last code leaf before the enum’s closing brace).

Added a **new regression test**:
- `EnumTrailingCommaWithMemberDeclarationsFormattingTest` verifies that `KtLintRuleEngine.format` on the reported enum snippet produces **no uncorrected errors** in the same run and formats as expected.

 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437→            )
438→
439→        @Deprecated(
440→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→
447→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
448→            CLASS,
449→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
450→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
451→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
452→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
453→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            WHEN_ENTRY,
455→        )
456→    }
457→}

1→# How to make new project release
 2→
 3→### Publishing a new release
 4→
 5→Note: These steps should be done directly in the pinterest/ktlint repository, not in your personal fork.
 6→
 7→1. Create a new branch (e.g. `0.50.0-prep`)
 8→2. Update `VERSION_NAME` with the new release version in the project root `gradle.properties`. Be sure to remove `-SNAPSHOT`.
 9→3. Update `CHANGELOG.md` to rename the `Unreleased` section to the new release name, following the `## [x.x.x] - YYYY-MM-DD` format.
10→4. Add the new release to the bottom of the `CHANGELOG.md` file.
11→5. Push your changes to the branch, and merge it to `master`.
12→6. Update your local `pinterest/ktlint` `master` branch; verify you see the `gradle.properties` and `CHANGELOG.md` changes locally.
13→7. Add a tag with the new release version, and push it directly to remote (e.g. `git tag 0.50.0 && git push origin 0.50.0`). This will kick off the [Release workflow](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/actions/workflows/release.yml).
14→9. Close and release the repo on Sonatype. (Only Pinterest employees can do this.)
15→10. Find the `<release>-update-refs` branch in the repo (created by the `.announce` script) and merge it.
16→11. Update `gradle.properties` with the new `SNAPSHOT` version, and add a new empty `Unreleased` section to the top of `CHANGELOG.md` and commit. (This can be done directly in the main repo or in your fork.)
17→

 10→class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleTest {
 11→    private val trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat =
 12→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule(
 13→            provider = { TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule() },
 14→            additionalRuleProviders = setOf(
 15→                // WrappingRule must be loaded in order to run TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule
 16→                RuleProvider { WrappingRule() },
 17→                // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
 18→                // correct.
 19→                RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
 20→            ),
 21→        )
 22→
 23→    @Test
 24→    fun `Given property allow trailing comma on declaration site is not set then remove trailing comma's`() {
 25→        val code =
 26→            """
 27→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int,)
 28→
 29→            class Foo2<A, B,> {}
 30→
 31→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
 32→                1, 2, -> "a"
 33→                else -> "b"
 34→            }
 35→
 36→            fun foo4() {
 37→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
 38→
 39→                val (x, y,) = bar()
 40→            }
 41→
 42→            val foo5: (Int, Int,) -> Int = 42
 43→
 44→            val foo6: (Int, Int,) -> Int = { foo, bar, -> foo * bar }
 45→            """.trimIndent()
 46→        val formattedCode =
 47→            """
 48→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int)
 49→
 50→            class Foo2<A, B> {}
 51→
 52→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
 53→                1, 2 -> "a"
 54→                else -> "b"
 55→            }
 56→
 57→            fun foo4() {
 58→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
 59→
 60→                val (x, y) = bar()
 61→            }
 62→
 63→            val foo5: (Int, Int) -> Int = 42
 64→
 65→            val foo6: (Int, Int) -> Int = { foo, bar -> foo * bar }
 66→            """.trimIndent()
 67→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
 68→            .hasLintViolations(
 69→                LintViolation(1, 29, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 70→                LintViolation(3, 16, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \">\""),
 71→                LintViolation(6, 9, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
 72→                LintViolation(13, 14, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 73→                LintViolation(16, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 74→                LintViolation(18, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 75→                LintViolation(18, 42, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
 76→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    @Test
 80→    fun `Given that the trailing comma is not allowed on declaration site then remove it from an parameter list when present`() {
 81→        val code =
 82→            """
 83→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int,)
 84→            data class Foo2(
 85→                val bar: Int, // The comma before the comment should be removed without removing the comment itself
 86→            )
 87→            data class Foo3(
 88→                val bar: Int, /* The comma before the comment should be removed without removing the comment itself */
 89→            )
 90→            """.trimIndent()
 91→        val formattedCode =
 92→            """
 93→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int)
 94→            data class Foo2(
 95→                val bar: Int // The comma before the comment should be removed without removing the comment itself
 96→            )
 97→            data class Foo3(
 98→                val bar: Int /* The comma before the comment should be removed without removing the comment itself */
 99→            )
100→            """.trimIndent()
101→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
102→            .withEditorConfigOverride(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY to false)
103→            .hasLintViolations(
104→                LintViolation(1, 29, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
105→                LintViolation(3, 17, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
106→                LintViolation(6, 17, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
107→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
108→    }

113→            """
114→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int)
115→            data class Foo2(
116→                val bar: Int // The comma should be inserted before the comment
117→            )
118→            data class Foo3(

121→            """.trimIndent()

127→            )

 17→ *
 18→ * ```
 19→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 20→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()

 23→ * fun `Some test`() {
 24→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 25→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 26→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 27→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 28→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 29→ * }
 30→ * ```
 31→ */
 32→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 33→public class KtLintAssertThat(

 35→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 36→     */

 44→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.

 55→     */
 56→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(

 67→     * silently overwritten.

 69→     * Example of usage:

 23→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 24→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 25→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 26→    val editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties,
 27→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 28→) {
 29→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 30→
 31→    init {
 32→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 33→    }
 34→
 35→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 36→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun executeRule(
 40→        rule: Rule,
 41→        fqRuleId: String,
 42→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 43→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 44→    ) {
 45→        rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 46→        rule.beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties)
 47→        this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 48→        rule.afterLastNode()
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 52→        node: ASTNode,
 53→        rule: Rule,
 54→        fqRuleId: String,
 55→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 56→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 57→    ) {
 58→        /**
 59→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLint.format]. So a new handler is to be built
 60→         * before visiting the nodes.
 61→         */
 62→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 63→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 64→            try {
 65→                suppressHandler.handle(node, fqRuleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 66→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 67→                }
 68→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 69→                    node
 70→                        .getChildren(null)
 71→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 72→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, fqRuleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 73→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 74→                                    childNode,
 75→                                    rule,
 76→                                    fqRuleId,
 77→                                    autoCorrect,
 78→                                    emit,
 79→                                )
 80→                            }
 81→                        }
 82→                }
 83→                suppressHandler.handle(node, fqRuleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 84→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 85→                }
 86→            } catch (e: Exception) {
 87→                if (autoCorrect) {
 88→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
 89→                    // original AST
 90→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
 91→                        0,
 92→                        0,
 93→                        fqRuleId,
 94→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
 95→                        e.cause ?: e,
 96→                    )
 97→                } else {
 98→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
 99→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
100→                        line,
101→                        col,
102→                        fqRuleId,
103→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
104→                        e.cause ?: e,
105→                    )
106→                }
107→            }
108→        }
109→    }
